ops.reason: Bridges the gap between the placement decision (Slide 17) and the unified icon outcome (Slide 18) — names the icon-unification problem as a distinct design question so the solution on Slide 18 lands as earned, not arbitrary.

slide.label: Icon System — Problem
slide.title: Placement wasn't the only thing broken
slide.content: Once the toolbar had a home, the next problem was plain: the icons inside it were built at different times, by different teams, with no shared style or logic.
issues: No consistent line weight or stroke style across tools
No shared visual logic between toolbar icons and procedure icons
Clinicians had to memorise each icon — nothing was self-explanatory

ops.reason: References slide. Inserted after original slide 17 (right after the icon-question slide above it, before the unified-icon 'After' slide 18) so the icon thread reads question -> inspiration -> resolution. Shows the external benchmarking that informed the icon-system direction; 3 empty image slots the designer fills with real product screenshots and named principles.

slide.label: Icon System — References
slide.title: What a consistent icon system looks like in practice
slide.description: Before drawing anything, I looked at products where icon systems work — many tools in one surface, nothing requiring memorisation.
